title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
An investigator discusses the deceased man's history with a colleague, noting that the man was a successful con artist who frequently changed his identities. He had operated under names such as Harry Castleton, Raymond Blair, Lawrence Dalton, and Roger Byron, though investigators could not prove these were the same person. The man had disappeared for fifteen to twenty years before dying on a carpet in Miss Pemaroo's living room. His wife, who identified the body, used the alias Melina Rival but her real name is Flora Gapp, a former actress and socialite. Investigations into the clocks found at the scene revealed that a gilded clock and a Dresden porcelain clock were purchased at the Pombal Market, while a silver clock was bought from a silversmith in Bournemouth by a woman in a green hat. When asked about a missing fourth clock, Huckleberry refuses to answer.

In a separate narrative, Colin Lamb, using the alias Douglas Weesby, meets Hilary Webber from the Cavendish Typewriting Agency at the Golden Daffodil Café. Hilary expresses intense fear of a detective who suspects her of murdering a man and Ina. She denies staging the scene or imitating Miss Pemaroo's voice on a phone call. Colin attempts to reassure her but suggests she may be hiding something. He tells her he is acting out of affection and believes her terror at the crime scene was genuine. The meeting concludes when Colin asks Hilary why she stole the clock inscribed with "Rosemary," a charge she denies.
